audit_items.proof_trace: `state = new size_t[L]` is allocated unconditionally at line 79. The initialization loop at lines 83–86 contains an early return (`if (in[i].size() == 0) return {};`) at line 84 that exits without executing the `delete[] state` guard at line 129. The only deallocation is on the normal exit path (line 129), so any call where an inner vector is empty leaks the entire `state` allocation.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker supplies a CSS stylesheet containing a parent-reference selector (`&`) whose resolved parent selector list contains an empty complex-selector group; this flows into `resolve_parent_refs()` → `permutateAlt(vars)` with an empty `vars` entry, triggering the early return at line 84 and leaking the heap-allocated `state` array on every such parse call, enabling cumulative memory exhaustion.
